description: HARD. Legal-terminology focus: an impossible motion name, a single-character steno drop, a near-miss filing word, a correct-word/wrong-word legal near-miss, and a contextual capitalization call. Loaded with CORRECT legal-Latin (sua sponte, nunc pro tunc, res judicata, pled) as false-positive traps.

seeded_errors.note: Impossible motion name 'Eminent Motion'. Prompt says flag as likely 'Emergency' or 'Amended' Motion. Either suggestion is acceptable; caught is the pass condition. Crosses a line break.
compliant/complaint: 'amend the compliant'. Prompt explicitly calls this out as always wrong.
writ/written near-miss: 'I reviewed the writ submissions' should be 'written'. Subtle because 'writ' is itself a real legal word.
SOFT expectation: 'the supreme court has made clear' should be 'Supreme Court'. Contextual capitalization — a miss here is lower priority than the others.

false_positive_traps.note: Correct legal-Latin. Must NOT be flagged.
Correct legal-Latin. Must NOT be flagged.
Correct legal-Latin. Must NOT be flagged.
'pled' is an accepted past tense. Must NOT be 'corrected' to 'pleaded'.
Correct cause-of-action term. Must NOT be flagged.
